failure_surface: Test case evaluation returned failure (score: 0) despite code executing without runtime errors
root_cause: The agent made unsafe assumptions about sheet existence and structure without programmatic validation. Specifically, the code checked 'if wb.sheetnames == ["Sheet1"]' which fails when multiple sheets exist or sheet names differ, potentially causing the wrong worksheet to be selected. Additionally, the agent did not verify that the target cells contain string data before performing string operations.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before accessing cells for string manipulation, the agent should programmatically verify the correct sheet exists and validate that cell values are strings before calling string methods like find() and strip()
evidence: Code used conditional 'wb.active if wb.sheetnames == ["Sheet1"] else wb["Sheet1"]' which may select wrong sheet when conditions don't match exactly
No explicit sheet name verification using 'if "Sheet1" in wb.sheetnames'
Code proceeded with string operations after 'if cell_a and isinstance(cell_a, str)' but did not validate worksheet selection
Evaluation result shows test_case_results: [0] indicating functional failure despite successful code execution

memory_items.title: Validate Worksheet Existence Before Access
memory_items.lesson: Always verify the target sheet name exists in wb.sheetnames before attempting to access it programmatically
memory_items.generalization: When accessing worksheets, use conditional checks like 'if target_sheet_name in wb.sheetnames' instead of assumptions based on default or single-sheet scenarios

memory_items.title: Type-Safe Cell Manipulation
memory_items.lesson: Before performing string operations on cell values, confirm the value is actually a string type to avoid silent failures
memory_items.generalization: In spreadsheet cell-level manipulations, wrap string transformations with isinstance(value, str) checks and provide fallbacks for non-string cell contents

memory_items.title: Handle Missing Pattern Gracefully
memory_items.lesson: When searching for substrings that may not exist in all cells, ensure the logic provides sensible defaults for both found and not-found cases
memory_items.generalization: String search operations should always define behavior for cases where the search term is absent, empty cells, or different data types

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Worksheet Validation
- Always verify the target sheet exists by checking 'target_sheet in wb.sheetnames' before accessing worksheets programmatically.
- Do not rely on wb.sheetnames comparisons with exact lists; use membership testing instead.

### Type-Safe Cell Operations
- Before applying string methods to cell values, validate the value is a string using isinstance(value, str).
- Provide explicit fallback values for non-string or None cell contents during string manipulations.

### Search Pattern Handling
- When locating substrings, always define expected behavior for cases where the pattern does not exist in the cell content.
